zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Henry De La Pomeroy於1291年10月4日出生在英國英格蘭可瓦爾時，他的父親Sir Henry De La Pomeroy26歲，母親Amice de Camville24歲。他於1325年1月與Joanna Moels在英國薩默塞特結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1367年10月22日在英國戴文貝里 波梅洛易享年76歲。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Pomeroy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin; mainly Devon and Dorset): habitational name from any of various places in northeastern France named with Old French pommeroie, pommeraie ‘apple orchard’ (collective of pomme ‘apple’). The name was established in Ireland in 1573.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Henry De La Pomeroy was born on 4 October 1291, in Cornwall, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Henry De La Pomeroy, was 26 and his mother, Amice de Camville, was 24. He married Joanna Moels in January 1325, in Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 22 October 1367, in Berry Pomeroy, Devon, England, at the age of 76.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Henry De La Pomeroy est né le 4 octobre 1291, à Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Henry De La Pomeroy, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Amice de Camville, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Joanna Moels en janvier 1325, à Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 22 octobre 1367, à Berry Pomeroy, Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 76 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Henry De La Pomeroyが1291年10月4日にイギリスイギリスコルンワルで生まれた時，父Sir Henry De La Pomeroyは26歳，母Amice de Camvilleは24歳であった。1325年1月にイギリスソメルセトでJoanna Moelsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1367年10月22日にイギリスデボンバーリー ポメロイで76歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Henry De La Pomeroy(이)가 1291년 10월 4일 영국 잉글랜드 코른월에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Henry De La Pomeroy(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Amice de Camville(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1325년 1월 영국 서머세트에서 Joanna Moels(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1367년 10월 22일 영국 데폰 베리 폼로이에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Henry De La Pomeroy родился 4 октября 1291 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Henry De La Pomeroy было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Amice de Camville, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Joanna Moels январь 1325 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 22 октября 1367 г. в местности бирри помирои, девон, Англия в возрасте 76 лет.
